summary: A chaotic yet thrilling 1978 audience recording with standout moments on Dancing in the Streets, Werewolves and a soaring Cry‑&‑Snow vocal by Jerry.
review: The show is famed as the “mescaline” performance – tempos wobble, vocals go wild, but the band feeds off that psychedelic energy. Highlights include Jerry’s heartfelt Cry & Snow (CR&S) vocal, Bobby’s fierce lead on Little Light/Supplication and the manic Dancing in the Streets jam, all captured on a surprisingly clear audience tape that still lets the crowd roar through the mix.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise its unique energy, while a few note sloppy tempos; recording is described as clear for an audience source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the tape as surprisingly clear with front‑center vocals and crowd ambience; no major dropouts reported.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set feels loose and chaotic, typical of a 1978 show – not as tight as ’77 dates but bursting with psychedelic spontaneity and strong crowd interaction.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is front‑center; praised for an emotional Cry & Snow vocal and noted for delivering clean, pure energy throughout the jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) is highlighted for wild vocals on Dancing in the Streets and a fierce lead on Little Light/Supplication, with several reviewers calling his performance a highlight.
